refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and the system to achieve and maintain energy efficiency performance such that measured energy_per_ton (energy_used / ton_moved) does not exceed the customer-defined E_target over the agreed evaluation period, under nominal operating conditions and across representative worksite scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-003 is refined to be measurable, time-bound, and scenario-aware. It explicitly ties the functional requirement to the acceptance criterion (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) and acknowledges that energy efficiency is a system-level property dependent on operator behavior, vehicle design, and operational context. The refinement separates the capability (enabling efficiency) from the performance outcome (achieving the target).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton across a representative set of worksite tasks (e.g., load-haul-dump cycles, grading passes) over the evaluation period. Compare measured values against E_target. Verify that the NGHE solution provides visibility, control, and feedback mechanisms that allow operators to optimize energy use.
sub_frs: FR-3.1: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy consumption telemetry to operators and remote monitoring systems.
FR-3.2: The NGHE solution shall provide operational guidance and control strategies that minimize energy consumption per unit of work output.
FR-3.3: The NGHE solution shall enable predictive energy modeling and performance tracking against E_target.
FR-3.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure that energy efficiency measures do not compromise safety or operational effectiveness.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized energy management system that monitors vehicle power consumption, optimizes drivetrain efficiency, and provides operator feedback via telemetry and in-cab displays.
DP-B: Distribute energy optimization logic across vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics) with local controllers and aggregate telemetry reporting.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach: vehicle-level energy monitoring and optimization with remote advisory services (tele-operator or cloud-based) that recommend operational adjustments based on real-time and historical data.
DP-D: Rely solely on operator training and manual control tuning without integrated energy management systems.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach combining vehicle-level energy monitoring and optimization with remote advisory services. Vehicle subsystems report energy metrics via Telemetry interface; remote services (Teleoperation or Safety Services) analyze performance and issue operational guidance via Tasking interface. This approach balances local responsiveness with centralized intelligence, supports both autonomous and tele-operated modes, and aligns with the NGHE architecture.

potential_sources: Energy optimization logic is decoupled from safety-critical control (safety remains independent).
Telemetry and Tasking interfaces are standard and support multiple consumers (operator displays, remote services, analytics).
Vehicle-level energy monitoring is independent of teleoperation mode; advisory services enhance but do not mandate control.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits low coupling because energy management is orthogonal to safety and mobility control. The hybrid architecture allows independent evolution of vehicle efficiency and remote advisory capabilities. Risk: if Telemetry or Tasking interfaces become saturated, energy advisory latency may increase; mitigation is interface prioritization and buffering.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle subsystems can reliably report energy consumption metrics with sufficient granularity and frequency.
Assumption: Remote advisory services have sufficient computational capacity and network latency to provide timely guidance.
Risk: Operator compliance with energy-efficiency recommendations may be low if they conflict with productivity or comfort preferences.
Risk: Energy optimization may interact with safety-critical functions (e.g., traction control, stability); requires careful validation.
Risk: High safety criticality (per tailoring factors) means energy optimization must never degrade safety margins; this constrains optimization aggressiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly state that energy optimization shall operate within safety-defined envelopes (e.g., minimum traction reserve, maximum thermal limits) and that safety-critical functions have priority over energy efficiency. Add requirement that advisory services shall not issue guidance that conflicts with active safety interventions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. For FR-3 (energy efficiency), the NGHE Solution owns the energy management architecture, including: (1) energy telemetry aggregation and normalization, (2) coordination of energy optimization across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, (3) energy performance tracking and reporting against E_target, and (4) enforcement of safety constraints on energy optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle subsystems report energy consumption (kWh, fuel consumption, power draw by subsystem) and operational state (load, speed, terrain, mode) via Telemetry. NGHE Solution consumes this data for energy modeling and advisory generation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution issues energy optimization guidance (e.g., recommended gear, throttle profile, route optimization) to Vehicle and Teleoperation via Tasking interface. Guidance is advisory unless explicitly mandated by safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services notifies NGHE Solution of active safety interventions or constraints (e.g., traction control engaged, thermal limits reached). NGHE Solution suspends or modulates energy optimization guidance in response.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with remote Teleoperation and advisory services via Network Link for real-time guidance and historical data upload.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of energy management architecture because it integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Energy efficiency is a system-level property that requires cross-module coordination. Minimal diffusion: energy telemetry collection is distributed to Vehicle, but aggregation and advisory logic are centralized in NGHE Solution.

Vehicle could own local energy optimization (e.g., transmission efficiency, engine load management) but cannot own system-level energy management because it lacks visibility into remote advisory services and cross-system constraints. High diffusion risk: energy optimization logic would be split between Vehicle (local) and NGHE Solution (system-level), creating coupling and maintenance burden.

Teleoperation could provide remote energy advisory services but should not own the energy management architecture because it is optional (system must function in autonomous mode) and is a consumer of energy data, not the authoritative source. Ownership here would create tight coupling between energy management and teleoperation availability.

Safety Services owns safety constraints but should not own energy optimization logic. High diffusion risk: energy management would be entangled with safety-critical functions, violating separation of concerns and complicating certification. Safety Services provides input (SafetyAlert) to energy management but does not own it.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy management is a system-level concern that requires integration of Vehicle telemetry, Teleoperation advisory, and Safety Services constraints. NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because it has visibility into all three domains and can enforce coherent energy optimization policy without creating diffusion or coupling risks.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic owner of energy management, it may become a bottleneck for energy-related changes. Mitigation: define clear sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., energy telemetry aggregator, advisory engine, performance tracker) with well-defined interfaces.

Risk: Energy optimization logic may inadvertently interact with safety-critical functions if not carefully isolated. Mitigation: enforce strict interface contracts between energy management and Safety Services; energy optimization shall never override safety decisions.

Risk: Remote advisory services (Teleoperation) may have high latency or unavailability, reducing energy optimization effectiveness. Mitigation: design NGHE Solution to operate in degraded mode (local optimization only) if remote services are unavailable.

Risk: Operator non-compliance with energy guidance may render energy management ineffective. Mitigation: design advisory interface to be non-intrusive and provide clear rationale for recommendations; track compliance and provide feedback.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify that NGHE Solution owns the energy management architecture but delegates energy telemetry collection to Vehicle and energy advisory delivery to Teleoperation. Define explicit sub-module boundaries (e.g., EnergyTelemetryAggregator, EnergyAdvisoryEngine, EnergyPerformanceTracker) within NGHE Solution to reduce monolithic risk and enable independent testing and evolution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid energy management with vehicle-level monitoring and remote advisory) directly addresses FR-3 (achieving energy efficiency target). The DP provides mechanisms for real-time energy visibility, optimization guidance, and performance tracking. The DP is feasible given the available interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and does not introduce new architectural dependencies. The DP respects the high safety criticality constraint by explicitly subordinating energy optimization to safety constraints.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk: Energy telemetry collection is distributed across Vehicle subsystems, but aggregation and advisory logic are centralized in NGHE Solution. This is acceptable because telemetry collection is a Vehicle responsibility (it owns the sensors and data sources) while energy management is a system-level responsibility.

Low risk: Energy advisory delivery is delegated to Teleoperation, but the advisory logic remains in NGHE Solution. This separation is clean and allows Teleoperation to be optional without compromising energy management capability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of energy management architecture. The module has clear ownership of energy telemetry aggregation, advisory generation, and performance tracking. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not require invention of new interfaces. Containment score of 95 for NGHE Solution reflects strong alignment between DP logic and module boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-3 is feasible through measurement of energy_per_ton over the evaluation period. NGHE Solution will provide energy performance tracking and reporting, enabling comparison against E_target. Verification does not require new instrumentation or interfaces beyond those already defined (Telemetry, Tasking).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of energy management responsibility is clear: NGHE Solution owns the architecture and coordination; Vehicle owns telemetry collection; Teleoperation owns advisory delivery (optional); Safety Services owns constraint enforcement. Each module has a well-defined role and interface.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to explicitly state that energy optimization shall operate within safety-defined envelopes and that safety-critical functions have priority.
Define sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., EnergyTelemetryAggregator, EnergyAdvisoryEngine, EnergyPerformanceTracker) to reduce monolithic risk.
Specify interface contracts for SafetyAlert to ensure that energy management respects safety constraints in real-time.
Define degraded-mode operation for NGHE Solution when remote advisory services (Teleoperation) are unavailable.

decision.justification: FR-3 is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-functional requirements. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (FR-3.1 through FR-3.4) that address distinct aspects of energy efficiency: telemetry, guidance, modeling, and safety integration. Each sub-FR requires its own FR-DP-IM decomposition to ensure complete and coherent design. The selected DP-C and assigned IM (NGHE Solution) are sound and pass all gates, but further decomposition is necessary to allocate responsibility for each sub-FR to specific modules and design parameters.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-3.1: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy consumption telemetry to operators and remote monitoring systems.
FR-3.2: The NGHE solution shall provide operational guidance and control strategies that minimize energy consumption per unit of work output.
FR-3.3: The NGHE solution shall enable predictive energy modeling and performance tracking against E_target.
FR-3.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure that energy efficiency measures do not compromise safety or operational effectiveness.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems can reliably report energy consumption metrics with sufficient granularity and frequency.
Remote advisory services have sufficient computational capacity and network latency to provide timely guidance.
Operators will comply with energy-efficiency recommendations if they are non-intrusive and well-justified.
Energy optimization can be logically separated from safety-critical functions and will not inadvertently degrade safety margins.
The evaluation period and worksite scenarios are representative of customer operational context.
open_risks: Operator compliance with energy-efficiency recommendations may be low if they conflict with productivity or comfort preferences.
Energy optimization may interact with safety-critical functions; requires careful validation and testing.
Remote advisory services may have high latency or unavailability, reducing energy optimization effectiveness.
NGHE Solution may become a monolithic owner of energy management, creating a bottleneck for changes.
Telemetry interface saturation may increase advisory latency if energy data volume is high.
evidence_needed: Specification of energy telemetry data types, granularity, and reporting frequency from Vehicle subsystems.
Specification of energy advisory guidance types and delivery mechanisms via Tasking interface.
Specification of SafetyAlert interface contract to ensure energy management respects safety constraints.
Specification of Network Link latency and bandwidth requirements for remote advisory services.
Validation plan for energy optimization interaction with safety-critical functions.
Operator interface design and usability testing for energy advisory guidance.
Measurement plan for energy_per_ton and comparison against E_target over evaluation period.
